transformation_type: rearrangement of vicinal diols or β-heterosubstituted alcohols to carbonyl compounds via cationic [1,2]-migration
mechanism_type: ionization/dehydration to carbocation followed by migratory shift and carbonyl formation
reactant_pattern_text: vicinal diol or 2-heterosubstituted alcohol capable of generating a cationic center
product_pattern_text: ketone, aldehyde, or ring-expanded carbonyl product
common_conditions: often uses protic or Lewis acids for pinacol rearrangements and mild leaving-group activation for semipinacol variants
description_short: Rearrangement manifold that converts vicinal diols or activated β-heterosubstituted alcohols into carbonyl compounds through a cationic [1,2]-shift and is widely used for ring expansion and stereocontrolled skeletal editing.

transformation_type: acid-mediated conversion of nitriles and alcohols or thiols into imino ether or imino thioether salts and downstream amidines or esters
mechanism_type: nitrilium-ion generation followed by heteroatom addition and proton transfer
reactant_pattern_text: nitrile + alcohol, phenol, or thiol under dry hydrogen halide conditions
product_pattern_text: imino ether hydrochloride, amidinium salt, ester, or imino thioether derivative
common_conditions: typically employs dry HCl at low temperature followed by hydrolysis, ammonia treatment, or intramolecular trapping
description_short: Classical nitrile activation method that turns nitriles into imino ether salts and provides a versatile gateway to amidines, esters, and related nitrogen-rich motifs.

transformation_type: buffered sodium chlorite oxidation of aldehydes to carboxylic acids
mechanism_type: chlorous-acid-mediated aldehyde oxidation with scavenging of hypochlorous-acid byproducts
reactant_pattern_text: aldehyde + NaClO2 under buffered aqueous conditions with alkene scavenger
common_conditions: typically buffered near pH 3–5 and paired with excess alkene scavenger to suppress chlorine-oxide side reactions
description_short: Reliable mild oxidation that converts aldehydes to carboxylic acids with sodium chlorite under buffered conditions while preserving many sensitive functional groups.

transformation_type: acylative activation of tertiary amine N-oxides to iminium intermediates and downstream α-functionalized amine products
mechanism_type: O-acylation of N-oxide followed by fragmentation or iminium capture/elimination
reactant_pattern_text: tertiary amine N-oxide + acylating agent such as Ac2O or TFAA
product_pattern_text: amide, aldehyde, alkene, iminium-derived reduction product, or oxygenated amine derivative
common_conditions: often uses preformed N-oxides and either classical acetic anhydride activation or trifluoroacetic anhydride in Potier-type variants
description_short: N-oxide activation chemistry that transforms tertiary amines into iminium equivalents and enables oxidative skeletal editing, fragmentation, or α-functionalization in complex alkaloid synthesis.

transformation_type: acid-promoted cyclocondensation of aromatic carbonyl compounds with 2,2-dialkoxyethylamines to form isoquinolines or tetrahydroisoquinolines
mechanism_type: Schiff-base or benzalaminoacetal formation followed by electrophilic cyclization and dealkoxylation
reactant_pattern_text: aromatic aldehyde or ketone + 2,2-dialkoxyethylamine or aminoacetal surrogate
common_conditions: typically forms a Schiff base or aminoacetal adduct that cyclizes under strong protic acid or Lewis acid heating conditions
description_short: Foundational isoquinoline-forming annulation that converts aromatic carbonyl compounds and aminoacetals into isoquinoline frameworks and supports multiple modified routes for complex alkaloid synthesis.
